pages.title: CabaCraft
pages.text: Minecraft 1.20.1 Fabric pack built on top of Origins. Three picks at world creation, in order:$(br)$(li)A $(l:races/overview)race$(/l) (e.g. Tortle, Tiefling, Dwarf).$(li)A $(l:classes/overview)primary class$(/l) (e.g. Mage, Engineer).$(li)A $(l:secondaries/overview)secondary class$(/l) (e.g. Brewer, Bard).

pages.title: What each pick does
pages.text: $(bold)Race$(/) decides what your body can do — shells, wings, breath, immunities, drawbacks.$(br2)$(bold)Primary class$(/) decides the large catalogue of items only you can craft — armor lines, vehicles, spell books, machines.$(br2)$(bold)Secondary class$(/) decides a smaller second catalogue — drinks, instruments, hats, gadgets.

pages.title: Where items come from
pages.text: Three sources only:$(br)$(li)Vanilla — drops, loot, base recipes.$(li)Your class catalogue — gear only your class can craft.$(li)A $(l:traders/overview)trader$(/l) — the cross-class shortcut. Other classes can buy your gear there for $(l:traders/runes)runes$(/l) scavenged from Mine Cells dungeons.$(br2)Vanilla recipes for class-owned items have been removed; the only way is class craft or trader.

pages.title: Factions
pages.text: Optional. After joining the world you may $(l:factions/overview)found or join a faction$(/l) — Desert, Island, or Knight. Faction leaders craft signature regalia no one else can.$(br2)A faction is bound to an FTB Teams party. The party owner is the leader.

pages.title: Switching your origin
pages.text: Race and class are permanent. Switching either requires a server admin.$(br2)Faction membership is not permanent — your party leader can claim or release a faction at any time.
